name: GPT-5.4
archDisplay: Undisclosed architecture (presumed dense transformer, reasoning-tuned); first general-purpose OpenAI model with native computer-use; ships in Thinking/Pro/mini/nano tiers
note: Flagship/'Thinking' tier of the GPT-5.4 family, confirmed real via TechCrunch and The Decoder launch coverage. GPT-5.4 Pro ($30/$180 per M, ARC-AGI-2 83.3%, GDPval 82%) and cheaper GPT-5.4 mini/nano also shipped the same month but are out of scope for this pass (not requested).

results.notes: Humanity's Last Exam, no tools (with-tools variant scores 52.1; Pro no-tools scores 42.7)
ARC-AGI-2; Pro variant scores 83.3 (cross-confirmed by deeplearning.ai's The Batch, which reported GPT-5.2 Pro at 54.2% vs GPT-5.4 Pro at 83.3%)
SWE-Bench Pro (harder variant of SWE-bench; not the base 'swe-bench' slug)

verificationNotes: Existence and March 5, 2026 launch date confirmed via TechCrunch ('OpenAI launches GPT-5.4 with Pro and Thinking versions') and Wikipedia. Pricing ($2.50/$15 per M) and 1M-token context (922K in / 128K out) cross-confirmed by The Decoder and LayerLens independently. Nine benchmark scores below (GDPval, FrontierMath T1-3/T4, GPQA Diamond, HLE, ARC-AGI-1/2, SWE-Bench Pro, BrowseComp) taken from The Decoder's launch-day writeup, which itself relays OpenAI's own announced figures (treated as self-reported per instructions). Could NOT confirm a separately-branded, separately-benchmarked 'GPT-5.4 (High)' tier distinct from the flagship numbers below or from the 'xhigh' config tracked by Artificial Analysis (AA composite Intelligence Index score 51 at launch, later cited as 57 in a follow-up AA post reflecting an index-methodology revision) — so no High-tier sibling was added; see 'uncertain'.
